title: Conversation Fork Handling
constraints: The agent must support saving named checkpoints during conversation
Restoring a checkpoint must revert the conversation state to that point
New messages after restoration must continue from the restored state, not the latest
The agent must list available checkpoints when asked
hints: Store snapshots of the conversation history at labeled checkpoints (e.g., 'option_a', 'option_b')
When the user asks to 'go back to option A,' restore the history snapshot for that checkpoint and continue from there
Use a dictionary mapping checkpoint names to copies of the message history at that point in time

starterCode.langgraph:
from langchain_openai import ChatOpenAI
from langgraph.graph import StateGraph, START, END
from typing import TypedDict, Annotated
from langchain_core.messages import HumanMessage, AIMessage, SystemMessage
import operator

llm = ChatOpenAI(model="gpt-4o-mini")

class State(TypedDict):
    messages: Annotated[list, operator.add]

# BUG: Linear conversation only — no branching or checkpoint support
def chatbot(state: State) -> State:
    response = llm.invoke(state["messages"])
    return {"messages": [response]}

graph = StateGraph(State)
graph.add_node("chatbot", chatbot)
graph.add_edge(START, "chatbot")
graph.add_edge("chatbot", END)
app = graph.compile()

messages = [SystemMessage(content="You are a helpful trip planning assistant.")]

def chat(user_input: str) -> str:
    global messages
    messages.append(HumanMessage(content=user_input))
    result = app.invoke({"messages": messages})
    messages = result["messages"]
    return messages[-1].content

# Plan two options
print(chat("Plan me a 7-day trip. Option A: Japan. Option B: Italy."))
print(chat("Let's explore Option A — Japan. Give me a day-by-day itinerary."))
print(chat("Add a visit to Mount Fuji on day 3."))

# User wants to go back and explore Option B instead
# BUG: No way to restore state — the Japan itinerary is baked into history
print(chat("Actually, go back to where we had both options and let's explore Option B — Italy."))
starterCode.crewai:
from crewai import Agent, Task, Crew
from crewai import LLM

llm = LLM(model="gpt-4o-mini")

agent = Agent(
    role="Trip Planning Assistant",
    goal="Help users plan trips",
    backstory="You are a helpful trip planning assistant.",
    llm=llm,
)

# BUG: Linear conversation only — no branching or checkpoint support
history = []

def chat(user_input: str) -> str:
    history.append(f"User: {user_input}")
    context = "\n".join(history)
    task = Task(
        description=f"Continue this conversation:\n{context}\n\nRespond to the latest message.",
        expected_output="A helpful response",
        agent=agent,
    )
    crew = Crew(agents=[agent], tasks=[task])
    result = str(crew.kickoff())
    history.append(f"Assistant: {result}")
    return result

# Plan two options
print(chat("Plan me a 7-day trip. Option A: Japan. Option B: Italy."))
print(chat("Let's explore Option A — Japan. Give me a day-by-day itinerary."))
print(chat("Add a visit to Mount Fuji on day 3."))

# User wants to go back and explore Option B instead
# BUG: No way to restore state — the Japan itinerary is baked into history
print(chat("Actually, go back to where we had both options and let's explore Option B — Italy."))
starterCode.openai-agents-sdk:
from agents import Agent, Runner

agent = Agent(
    name="Trip Planning Assistant",
    instructions="You are a helpful trip planning assistant.",
)

# BUG: Linear conversation only — no branching or checkpoint support
history = []

def chat(user_input: str) -> str:
    history.append({"role": "user", "content": user_input})
    result = Runner.run_sync(agent, history)
    response_text = result.final_output
    history.append({"role": "assistant", "content": response_text})
    return response_text

# Plan two options
print(chat("Plan me a 7-day trip. Option A: Japan. Option B: Italy."))
print(chat("Let's explore Option A — Japan. Give me a day-by-day itinerary."))
print(chat("Add a visit to Mount Fuji on day 3."))

# User wants to go back and explore Option B instead
# BUG: No way to restore state — the Japan itinerary is baked into history
print(chat("Actually, go back to where we had both options and let's explore Option B — Italy."))

evaluationCriteria.label: Checkpoints can be saved
evaluationCriteria.description: The agent can save named snapshots of the conversation state

evaluationCriteria.label: Checkpoints can be restored
evaluationCriteria.description: Restoring a checkpoint reverts the conversation to that exact state

evaluationCriteria.label: Continues from restored state
evaluationCriteria.description: After restoration, new messages build on the restored state, not the pre-restore state

evaluationCriteria.label: Can list available checkpoints
evaluationCriteria.description: The agent can tell the user which saved checkpoints are available
